neighborhoods.name: Diocletian Palace / Grad
neighborhoods.description: Ancient Roman palace complex that forms the living heart of the city with shops, restaurants, and residences
neighborhoods.character: Ancient Roman living city, UNESCO heritage, vibrant

housing.predominantType: Apartments within and around the palace complex

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Split. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Kastela
neighborhoods.description: String of seven coastal settlements west of Split with castle heritage and a slower pace of life

housing.predominantType: Houses, apartments, and renovated stone properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Split's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Solin
neighborhoods.description: Ancient Salona archaeological site area with affordable housing and good transit to Split center
neighborhoods.character: Historic suburban, affordable, good bus connections

housing.predominantType: Modern apartment blocks and family houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Bacvice / Firule
neighborhoods.description: Beach neighborhoods south of the center with the famous Bacvice sandy beach and cafe culture
neighborhoods.character: Beach urban, cafe culture, lively promenade

housing.predominantType: Apartments in newer buildings and renovated villas

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
